rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHERMAN ALLEN SODERLUND
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/27/2020. 1) No proof of service is on file. 2) An original bank statement verifying the ending balance of the estate cash must be filed. See LRSF 14.62 & 14.93D. 3) No election by the personal representative of the surviving spouse's estate has been filed. An ex parte petition for approval of a late election must be filed. See Probate Code section 13502(b) & LRSF 14.67(L)(5). 4) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) Was notice required to be given to any public entity under Probate Code section 9201? B) The information required by CRC 7.250(a)(3) must be provided for the reported sale of the estate real property. C) The account period must start with the date of death. See LRSF 14.59. D) Were Shane Soderlund and Sven Soderlund paying rents to the petitioner or sharing expenses before the Gennessee property was sold? E) The attorney's statutory fee calculation is slightly incorrect. F) If the estate property represents the decedent's community property interest, why is it proposed to be distributed to the decedent's children? See Probate Code section 6401(a). G) Why is stock of Bank of America, which was reportedly sold in 1999, listed on the schedules of the beginning asset on hand and losses on sale? H) The petition alleges that the estate cash has been kept in interest-bearing accounts, but the schedule of receipts shows no payment of interest. I) The costs of sale of the Gennesse property must be itemized on the disbursement schedule. See Probate Code section 1062(b). J) Once the late election to subject the property passing to the surviving spouse's estate to administration has been approved, an allegation required by CRC 7.652(b) must be provided.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHIA MING SHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/20/2020 (no earlier date available). 1) Notice was not timely published. 2) The proof of publication states the petition requests that the decedent's will be admitted to probate, but the petition alleges the decedent died intestate. 3) An amended duties and liabilities of personal representative must be filed to provide the petitioner's personal telephone number. 4) A certified copy of letters of administration issued by the Guam court must be filed. 5) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) Item 3c of the petition must be completed. B) Did the petitioner have a personal or business relationship with the decedent? C) Applicable boxes at Items 5a(1)-(4) must be checked. D) Did Hui Fang Shen, Chi Jung Shen, and Chi Hwa Shen designate the P.O. Box addresses to receive notice? See Probate Code section 1220(a)(3) (requiring notice to be given to a beneficiary's place of business or residence). E) Item 8 of the petition refers to one of the decedent's children as Chi "Teh" Shen, while the proof of service of notice refers to him as Chi "Ti" Shen. Which name is accurate?
